text:
There was once a sweet old cat named Felicity.


One sunny afternoon, Felicity lay nestled under the shade of some ancient oak trees. She liked nothing more than soaking up the gentle warmth and listening to birds tweet.

Suddenly, she heard something fluttering nearby. Curious, she moved quietly toward the noise.

There fluttered dozens of butterflies!

Eating them was strange. They tasted like mint. Maybe it was worth it?

Two hawks watched from sky-high.

One hawk screeched loudly. It seemed afraid.

Felicity felt bold. With soft paws, she fluttered closer.

That hawk! Think swift circles.

Felicity spun.

They zigzagged!

But she could fly higher.

With a flap fest.

Those big birds never felt love!



Oh feathers!

Felicity chased!

But a branch!

With sudden swoop!

Down came stone!

No game!

Just some sparrows.

She picked them!

So tasty!

Now fly away!

Fly away!
